page: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities. The instance scores in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also demonstrates strong performance in text processing and text generation for small language models. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core score, and software compilation, fall within the average range.

A notable trade-off is observed in memory performance. While cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is average, large-block uncached read-only memory bandwidth and the PassMark memory score fall into the bottom 25%. This indicates potential bottlenecks for memory-bound workloads processing large datasets. Qualitatively, the instance offers compute-focused resource density, making it cost-efficient for CPU-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for batch processing, web serving, single-threaded applications, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core benchmarks, alongside strong text processing and small-model text generation. However, it exhibits weaker performance in large-block uncached memory read bandwidth. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without high memory overhead. It is suited for web serving, batch processing, and single-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for high-performance single-threaded and batch workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for batch processing, web serving, and single-threaded workloads
